onboarding.question:
1. [PM] AC uses vague language: "clear" — two implementers would interpret this differently. — Replace "clear" with a concrete, measurable criterion.
2. [PM] AC uses vague language: "clear" — two implementers would interpret this differently. — Replace "clear" with a concrete, measurable criterion.
3. [PM] AC uses undefined timing: "after inactivity" without a numeric value in seconds or minutes. — Replace "after inactivity" with a specific duration (e.g. "within 200ms", "after 60 seconds of inactivity").
4. [PM] AC uses undefined timing: "immediately" without a numeric value in seconds or minutes. — Replace "immediately" with a specific duration (e.g. "within 200ms", "after 60 seconds of inactivity").
onboarding.recommendations:
1. My recommendation: Replace "a clear sign-up or sign-in call-to-action" (AC 10) with "a sign-up or sign-in call-to-action rendered as a tappable button within the system message."
→ Rationale: "Clear" is subjective — specifying that the CTA is a tappable button gives the designer a concrete component to implement and the engineer a testable criterion.
→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

2. My recommendation: Replace "a clear call-to-action to sign up or sign in" (AC 12) with "a tappable 'Sign in' button that opens the Auth Sheet."
→ Rationale: Every CTA in this spec resolves to the Auth Sheet — making that explicit eliminates ambiguity for both the designer and the engineer without constraining the visual treatment.
→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

3. My recommendation: Replace "after inactivity" in AC 8 with "after 50 minutes of cumulative inactivity" — which is 10 minutes before the 60-minute session expiry established in AC 23.
→ Rationale: The 60-minute session TTL is already locked in AC 23; the warning fires at the 50-minute mark, giving users exactly the 10-minute window the spec describes without introducing a new timing variable.
→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

4. My recommendation: Replace "immediately" in AC 20 with "within one render cycle of the initial page load — before any user interaction is possible."
→ Rationale: The intent is that the user never sees an authenticated state that then flips to logged-out; a render-cycle bound is testable and matches the spirit of the requirement without introducing an arbitrary millisecond value that engineering would likely override anyway.
→ Note: Pending your approval — say yes to apply to the product spec

Say *yes* to apply these to the product spec and continue design, or reply to adjust any recommendation.
